raw_consumer_statement:
This is essentially what happened from my point of view, my ex-wife called into Barclays Bank to access my credit card account. An account that I had only for emergencies. They allowed her to add herself to the account, change the account phone number, change the account address, and change the account email address. She then proceeded to use the account and charged approx. {$2500.00} by the time I caught it. 
I figured this all out around the end of the first week XX/XX/year>2025. I proceeded to call Barclays and spoke to a representative who gave me a hard time trying to just access the account, eventually agreeing to report it as fraudulent. Barclays continued to roll the balance and expected me to pay off the balance, I refused to claiming it was all fraud. They said it would all be resolved within XX/XX/XXXX days. I called back aboutXX/XX/XXXX days in when I started getting delinquent payment notices. Then at the end of the next XX/XX/XXXX days, same thing. I called back again and requested a call back from a supervisor. She said she would get it all cleared up and nothing happened. This went on until XX/XX/year>2025, until I got a sympathetic gentleman on the phone who was able to remove the balance. 
However, my credit score has gone from mid XXXXXX/XX/XXXX to currently a XXXX. I can not refi my HELOC because of the drop in my score and has wreaked havoc on my credit. 
I am sure there is a lot of things I am missing in the middle, like I was promised multiple times that Barclays would clear up my credit, but nothing has happened yet. We are now in mid XXXX and still nothing. This is not right. They allowed someone to access my credit card who was not authorized. They allowed my credit to be affected. 
I need help.
